title: Pathlib, Content Discovery, Roots, Symlinks, and Admission · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded content-discovery manifest and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Use Path and PurePath operations with explicit platform, resolution, relative, and I/O semantics instead of string slicing.
Declare roots, extensions, ignores, hidden files, drafts, ordering, maximum files, depth, and total bytes before traversal.
Admit or reject symlinks using resolved ancestry, cycle handling, file type, and ownership policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-1vjx7a" that uses case 4816 to build a bounded content manifest with explicit rejection, root, and collision evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Discovery Manifest.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Discovery Manifest into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Discovery Manifest, keep Discovery Manifest connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-discovery-manifest-1vjx7a
# Competency: ssg-discovery-manifest.
# Case 4816: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_discovery_manifest_1vjx7a(discovered: int = 12, admitted: int = 10, rejected: int = 2, root_escapes: int = 0, route_collisions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1vjx7a = "1vjx7a-a7xjv1-4816"
    assert evidence_variant_1vjx7a
    evidence_axes_1vjx7a = (
        "reader1vjx7a",
        "source4816",
        "parsea7xjv1",
        "route1vjx7a4816",
        "artifact48161vjx7a",
        "recoverya7xjv14816",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1vjx7a)) == 6
    if min(discovered, admitted, rejected) < 0:
        return False, discovered, "discovery-count"
    if admitted + rejected != discovered:
        return False, admitted, "discovery-reconciliation"
    if root_escapes > 0:
        return False, root_escapes, "root-escape"
    if route_collisions > 0:
        return False, route_collisions, "route-collision"
    assert admitted + rejected == discovered
    return True, admitted, "admitted-content-manifest"
